_note: Curated by hand. To add an entry, edit this file and open a PR. Order matters — top entries surface first.

entries.name: Superpowers
entries.description: Brainstorming, planning, debugging, code-review, and skill-creation skills bundled into one official plugin.
entries.whyRecommended: The plugin most Claude Code power users install first — covers thinking, building, and shipping in one place.

entries.name: Firecrawl
entries.description: Search, scrape, and crawl any web page through the Firecrawl API — full markdown extraction, structured data, and screenshots.
entries.whyRecommended: Best-in-class web access MCP. Drops in cleanly and handles JS-heavy pages other scrapers can't touch.

entries.name: claude-mem
entries.description: Persistent memory plugin — Claude remembers conversations, decisions, and project context across sessions.
entries.whyRecommended: Solves the cold-start problem on long-running projects. Lightweight enough to leave on always.

entries.name: gstack
entries.description: Workflow plugin: /browse, /qa, /ship, /investigate, /design-review, /retro, /codex, /careful, /freeze, and more.
entries.whyRecommended: A full PM/eng workflow toolkit packaged as one plugin. Use one or two skills, or the whole stack.

entries.name: Supermemory
entries.description: Index your codebase or notes into Supermemory and search them from inside Claude Code.
entries.whyRecommended: Cross-session memory backed by a real vector store — alternative to claude-mem when you want a hosted backend.

entries.name: Obsidian
entries.description: Read and edit your Obsidian vault from Claude Code — wikilinks, frontmatter, callouts, JSON Canvas.
entries.whyRecommended: Built specifically for Obsidian's flavored markdown. Worth installing the moment you have a vault.

entries.name: Frontend Design
entries.description: Generates production-grade React/Tailwind/shadcn components with high design quality, not generic AI slop.
entries.whyRecommended: When you want UI that looks like a designer touched it, not a scaffold. Pairs well with Superpowers.

entries.name: Agent SDK Dev
entries.description: Helper plugin for building applications on the Claude Agent SDK — scaffolds, verifies, and tests SDK apps.
entries.whyRecommended: Required if you're building agents on top of Claude. Skip if you're not.

entries.name: GitHub MCP
entries.description: Official GitHub MCP server — read issues, PRs, files, and repo metadata directly from Claude Code.
entries.whyRecommended: Maintained by GitHub themselves. Authenticated via PAT or GitHub App, scoped permissions.

entries.name: Filesystem MCP
entries.description: Reference MCP server for reading/writing files in a sandboxed directory. Anthropic's canonical example.
entries.whyRecommended: Reference implementation for the spec. Tiny, easy to audit, useful when you want filesystem access scoped to one project.

entries.name: Fetch MCP
entries.description: Lightweight HTTP fetch as an MCP — when Firecrawl is overkill but you still need one URL pulled.
entries.whyRecommended: Tiny, dependency-free, no API keys. The minimum-viable web access MCP.

entries.name: Puppeteer MCP
entries.description: Drive a real Chromium instance from Claude Code — clicks, screenshots, form fills, full DOM access.
entries.whyRecommended: When you need actual browser behavior, not just markdown. Heavier than Fetch but unmatched for QA + scraping interactive sites.

entries.name: Postgres MCP
entries.description: Read-only Postgres access — Claude can query your database with your auth, can't write or DDL by default.
entries.whyRecommended: Safe-by-default schema and read access. Pair with a separate read-write tool only when you mean it.

entries.name: SQLite MCP
entries.description: Local SQLite access for prototyping. Drop in a .db file and Claude can introspect, query, and write.
entries.whyRecommended: The fastest way to give Claude a structured store for a one-off project. No server, no auth.

entries.name: Anthropic Skills
entries.description: Anthropic's official skill bundle: brand-guidelines, pdf, pptx, xlsx, docx, mcp-builder, skill-creator, web-artifacts-builder.
entries.whyRecommended: Document I/O, theming, and skill scaffolding from the source. Pair with Superpowers for full coverage.

entries.name: Code Security Reviewer
entries.description: Subagent that reviews recently changed code for both quality issues and security vulnerabilities.
entries.whyRecommended: Catches injection patterns, auth flaws, and unsafe deps before you ship. Trigger automatically post-commit.

entries.name: Design Critique
entries.description: Designer's-eye review of mockups, screenshots, and live pages — hierarchy, spacing, contrast, accessibility.
entries.whyRecommended: Catches AI-slop UI patterns and the spacing/alignment gaps that ship-ready features still have.

entries.name: /ship
entries.description: Slash command that runs tests, bumps VERSION, updates CHANGELOG, commits, pushes, and opens a PR — in one go.
entries.whyRecommended: Compresses the boring 8-step ship sequence into one verb. Hard to live without after a week.

entries.name: /review
entries.description: Pre-merge PR review against the base branch — flags SQL injection, conditional side effects, LLM trust boundary issues.
entries.whyRecommended: Calibrated for the bugs that actually slip through reviews, not the lint-level stuff.

entries.name: Stop Chime
entries.description: Plays a sound when Claude finishes its turn. Tiny example showing how to wire a Stop hook.
entries.whyRecommended: Lets you walk away during long tasks. Also a great template for your first hook.
